other_observers: none; I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: The bird was approximately the size and shape of a Yellow-rumped Warbler.  It was fairly inactive for a warbler.   Its cap, nape, and mantle were a dull, uniform, unstreaked yellow-green. The face was that same color, but with a broken bright yellow eye ring and a partial bright yellow collar.  The throat and breast were bright yellow, unmarked except for a bit of coarse, low contrast streaking at the sides of the breast. The belly and undertail coverts were clean white.  The bird had bold white wingbars.Behaviors: foraging in the foliage of a tree, like a warbler, but less active than most warblersCall: not heardPlumage: adult
